post.content: there is an owl outside. i can hear it. who who who.

soper.content: 1 free spin feature or 1 hold-and-win feature
Create a slot with a self-feeding narrative loop.
Theme approach Classic detective-for-hire base game

Flow: client arrives ->
free spins = learning the case ->
new reel environment (investigation) ->
trigger again ->
free spins = solving the case ->
return to office

**A two-act investigation arc**.

1- anticipation
2- resolution
Each beat becomes a series of story phases.
Classic detective in the office set up
Base game is reels over the detective's office space. We can see the office door closed with the inverted detective gold label over the glass. The atmosphere is dark, the glass itself seems tinted.

We do not see the detective because 
### they are you.
Trigger event: Cash coin event A shadowy figure enters thought the door:
Player is awarded n free spins. This is the case discovery phase.

Symbols on the reels are replaced by clues cards representing different artifacts related to a detective inquiry:

    cheating spouse
    stolen inheritance
    missing person
    lost item
    bad business

magnifying glass
revolver
fedora
newspaper
safe

Case symbols might become:

photograph
letter
knife
footprint
train ticket
the reels feel like evidence gathering.

silhouette client is secretly doing a lot of work
Because it creates infinite case seeds.

You can vary:

client personality
client urgency
client wealth
client tone

Examples:

    nervous widow

    rich businessman

    desperate teenager

    shady gangster

You instantly get tone variation.

narrative reset problem
Many narrative slots fail because they don?t explain why the story resets.  

Solution is elegant:

Detective office
waiting for next client

every reset is:

_a new case_

the same trick used by:

    detective shows

    medical dramas

    police procedurals

Each episode = new problem.
the look of the environment is unique to the case and the symbol pack is changed
extremely effective because it creates the illusion of progress without changing the math

Structurally:

Office reels
->
Case environment reels
->
Resolution event
->
Office reset

The player feels like they moved somewhere, even though mechanically it's the same reels.

pseudo-progression design.
